manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: friend, difference, report, dad, accident, middle, drink, age, sister.
Your opponent's team words are: colonel, gold, bank, street, ladies, fun, couple, record.
Distractor words are: bar, sir, mom, news, plane, country, order.
The kill words are: kind.

message.content:
.CLUE: generation
TARGETS: dad, sister, age, difference
message.reasoning:
We need to provide a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words. Must follow format exactly. Provide CLUE: WORD, TARGETS: list of target words (comma separated). Avoid using any words from any list. The clue cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them. Provide minimal text.

We need to decide which words we can clue together. Our team words: friend, difference, report, dad, accident, middle, drink... etc.

We can try to clue "family" maybe relates to dad, sister, friend? Friend not family. But dad and sister are family; "family" is not a word in lists, okay. Could also relate to "age" maybe? Family ages? Eh.

Maybe "birthday": relates to age, dad? Not exactly. Also "birthday" is not in list, okay. Could also clue "midlife": middle, age, difference? But midlife not in list.

We need to be careful about kill word "kind". Clue cannot be "kind" obviously.

We can do clue "health": relates to accident, drink, report? Maybe not.

Goal is to maximize number of words we can get guessed correctly but not accidental opponent words. However we only need to provide clue and targets; not actually playing.

Thus we just need to produce a valid clue and list of our team's target words we think the clue relates to.

Pick maybe "family": targets: dad, sister. Could also include friend? Not exactly family but could be "friend" as chosen family. Might be okay but ambiguous.

Alternatively "medical": relates to accident, report (medical report), age (medical age), drink? Not.

"midnight": middle, drink? Could be "midnight drink"? Not.

"report" maybe clue "news": but news is a distractor word, can't be used as clue? Actually clue cannot be a word in any list. "news" is a distractor word, can't be clue. So avoid.

Maybe "generation": relates to dad, sister, age, difference? Could be.

"generation": not in lists. So clue: generation. Targets: dad, sister, age, difference? Possibly.

But we must ensure clue is semantically related to the target words. Generation is about age differences, family. That could work.

Let's choose "generation". Provide targets: dad, sister, age, difference.

Check if "generation" contains parts of any word? No. Good.

Format:

CLUE: generation
TARGETS: dad, sister, age, difference

Make sure comma-separated list but no spaces after commas? It said comma-separated list; example earlier includes spaces maybe. We'll include spaces.

Thus final answer
